content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The implementation is well-structured: a new `task_prompt_contract` module defines canonical section names, a versioned HTML-comment marker for drift detection, and stage consumer guidance that's conditionally injected by the workflow engine. The prompt generator is rewritten to emit all eight contract sections in stable order, and the template catalog correctly treats the new placeholder as optional. Tests cover marker stability, detection, section ordering, determinism, and the engine integration path. All 154 relevant tests pass (the lone tmux test failure is pre-existing). Clippy warnings are all pre-existing and unrelated. Two issues found: (1) a minor formatting bug where a period sits inside a backtick code span in the guidance text, and (2) the `already_planned_elsewhere` field has no production data source, so the contract section is inert in real usage.

## Amendments

1. In `stage_consumer_guidance()` (task_prompt_contract.rs:63), the period is inside the backtick code span: `` `bead_execution_prompt/1.` ``. Move it outside: change the format string from `"{BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_NAME}/{}.`"` to `"{BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_NAME}/{}`."`  so the rendered text is `` `bead_execution_prompt/1`. `` — otherwise LLM consumers or any future parser that extracts the contract identifier from this guidance text would include the trailing period as part of the name.
   Rationale: Correctness: the period is semantically punctuation, not part of the contract version identifier. Placing it inside the code span conflates data with prose.
2. The `already_planned_elsewhere` field on `BeadProjectContext` is always hardcoded to `Vec::new()` in the only production construction site (cli/project.rs:340). The bead data model has no corresponding field to populate it. This means the `Already Planned Elsewhere` contract section will always render its default fallback text in production. Consider either (a) wiring it to bead dependency data (e.g. non-blocking dependencies), (b) parsing related beads from the milestone bundle, or (c) documenting it as a placeholder for future data wiring so the gap is intentional and tracked.
   Rationale: The contract defines a section that has no production data source. Tests exercise it with manually-injected data, masking the fact that real users will never see meaningful content in this section. If this is intentional future work, the code should document that.
